message:
chore(release): docs.rs metadata per crate + rustfmt.toml (br-wskz + br-thty)

Two release-day polish slices in one commit — both additive,
no source/test changes, both compile-verified under rch.

br-wskz (HIGH): [package.metadata.docs.rs] added to all 12
crates. Without this block, docs.rs defaults to:
  - --no-default-features build
  - Linux x86_64 target only

For fp-runtime (asupersync feature) and fp-conformance (asupersync
feature via fp-runtime/asupersync) this means the feature-gated
API items disappear from the rendered docs. When br-fd90's SQL
backend epic ships sql-sqlite / sql-postgresql / sql-mysql
features, those trait impls would also fail to render.

Per-crate block:
  [package.metadata.docs.rs]
  all-features = true                               # build every feature
  rustdoc-args = ["--cfg", "docsrs"]                # activate doc_cfg badges
  targets = ["x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"]            # Linux-primary docs

all-features=true is safe for featureless crates (no-op). For the
feature-gated crates, it means docs.rs shows the complete public
surface. rustdoc-args exposes #[doc(cfg(feature = "X"))]
annotations as "only on crate feature X" badges in the rendered
docs (when paired with `#![cfg_attr(docsrs, feature(doc_cfg))]`
in each crate's lib.rs — follow-up slice).

br-thty (HIGH): rustfmt.toml at repo root captures style
opinions that were implicit before:
  edition = "2024"                  # matches workspace.package
  max_width = 100                   # industry-standard Rust width
  hard_tabs = false, tab_spaces = 4
  newline_style = "Unix"
  use_small_heuristics = "Default"
  imports_granularity = "Crate"     # groups per-crate imports
  group_imports = "StdExternalCrate" # std / external / crate separation
  reorder_imports = true, reorder_modules = true
  unstable_features = true          # we're on pinned nightly via br-1d9y

Prevents swarm-style-drift where each agent's local rustfmt
default would silently reformat the same file three different
ways. CI's existing `cargo fmt --check` step now enforces these
explicit opinions rather than rustfmt defaults.

No bulk-reformat commit landed as part of this — committing just
the config so future commits comply. If a team-wide reformat is
desired, it should be a separate `chore: apply rustfmt.toml
opinions` commit that reviewers can fast-scroll past.

Verified under rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenpandas_cc):
  cargo check --workspace → Finished dev in 1m 07s, 0 errors.

Related:
- br-h8a8 (workspace.package — shipped) — sibling release-metadata.
- br-4clx (release workflow — open) — docs.rs metadata is consumed
  when the release workflow triggers first cargo publish.
- br-7cfm (rustdoc contract — shipped by swarm) — the #[doc(cfg)]
  annotation sweep activated by rustdoc-args=["--cfg","docsrs"]
  is a follow-up slice on top of the rustdoc crate-root setup
  7cfm landed.
- br-1d9y (nightly date pin — shipped by swarm) — rustfmt
  unstable_features = true requires nightly; we're pinned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
